title: asplenium sarelii subsp. sarelii
intro: Asplenium sarelii subsp. sarelii, also known as Sareli's maidenhair spleenwort, is a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae, characterized by its delicate fronds and terrestrial growth habit. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, where it is believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Asplenium sarelii subsp. sarelii include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in combination with other herbs, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
